title: Fixed Background Section
description: Create a section with background-attachment: fixed for a parallax-like effect.
hint: Set background-attachment: fixed on a section with a background-image.
solution: .parallax-section { height: 300px; background-image: url('bg.jpg'); background-size: cover; background-attachment: fixed; background-position: center; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: white; }

title: Simple Perspective Scroll
description: Create a container with perspective: 1px and a background that scrolls slower.
hint: Use perspective on wrapper, translateZ on background.
solution: .wrapper { height: 300px; overflow-y: auto; perspective: 1px; } .bg { position: absolute; inset: 0; transform: translateZ(-1px) scale(2); } .content { transform: translateZ(0); position: relative; }

title: Three-Layer Parallax Scene
description: Build a scene with background, midground, and foreground that all scroll at different speeds.
hint: Use translateZ values of -3, -1, and 0 with appropriate scale compensation.
solution: .scene { perspective: 1px; height: 400px; overflow-y: auto; transform-style: preserve-3d; } .bg { transform: translateZ(-3px) scale(4); } .mid { transform: translateZ(-1px) scale(2); } .fg { transform: translateZ(0); }

title: Scroll-Triggered Parallax with CSS+JS
description: Create a parallax effect where elements move at different speeds based on scroll position, using minimal JS.
hint: Use Intersection Observer or scroll event with requestAnimationFrame.
solution: window.addEventListener('scroll', () => { const scrolled = window.scrollY; document.querySelectorAll('.parallax-layer').forEach((layer, i) => { const speed = 0.3 + i * 0.2; layer.style.transform = `translateY(${scrolled * speed}px)`; }); }, { passive: true });
